conversation_summaries.overall_pre_game: I felt most connected to G, E, and D, as our conversations were deeply personal and aligned on family goals and game strategies, making me feel a strong sense of trust and camaraderie— for instance, with G, we shared stories about family vacations and agreed on coordinating roles early, while with E and D, we brainstormed cooperative tactics like holding back on the prize pool and organizing group votes. In contrast, I felt least connected to B, as our discussion stayed mostly on shared hardships in our hometowns without delving into game plans or building any real alliance. Yes, I made several agreements, such as forming a huddle with J, approaching H with F, and setting a prize pool limit with D, which solidified early alliances. Going into the game, I trust G, E, and D based on our open communications, but I don't fully trust others like B or I yet, as our interactions felt less committed.
